_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of Risk Adaptive (Adaptable) Access Controls (RAdAC)
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Knowledge SP-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-007 (SP, cosine: 0.493), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.490).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.5/10 (raw 0.456). Shared vocabulary: [access, controls, knowledge]. NICE-only terms [adaptable, adaptive, radac, risk] vs WIDAI-only terms [architectural, classification, data, design, enforce, labeling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.837 (P=0.825, R=0.850). R exceeds P by 0.025 — the WIDAI KSA's 21 words are well-captured by the NICE element's 8 words, but the NICE element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Knowledge of Risk Adaptive (Adaptable) Access Controls (RAdAC)" and "Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural co" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.506 > STS 0.456 (gap +0.050). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [access, controls, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.4%. Shared tokens: [access, controls, knowledge]. NICE-only: [adaptable, adaptive, radac, risk]. WIDAI-only: [architectural, classification, data, design, enforce, labeling].
